proof_trace: In the error path at L682-686 of createMetazoneMappings, when the UVector constructor sets a failure status, line 684 calls deleteOlsonToMetaMappingEntry(entry) which internally calls uprv_free(entry), then line 685 immediately calls uprv_free(entry) again ‚ÄĒ a confirmed double-free. The entry has not yet been inserted into mzMappings (addElement at L690 is unreached), so no other path would free it.
attack_scenario: An attacker who can induce memory pressure causing the UVector internal allocation to fail will trigger this error path via getMetazoneID ‚Üí getMetazoneMappings ‚Üí createMetazoneMappings, achieving a double-free that enables heap corruption and potential arbitrary code execution.

proof_trace: deleteEntry() frees TransliteratorEntry including its compoundFilter member (destructor L483: delete compoundFilter), but instantiateEntry() at L1266 (ALIAS) and L1299 (COMPOUND_RBT) passes entry->compoundFilter as a raw non-owning pointer to TransliteratorAlias, which stores it without copying. In createBasicInstance() (translit.cpp), registryMutex is released after get() (line 983) but before alias->create() (line 1019); a concurrent Transliterator::unregister() call within that window triggers deleteEntry() on the same entry, freeing compoundFilter while the alias still holds a dangling pointer. The source comment at lines 30-36 of transreg.cpp explicitly notes that BoundsChecker reported dangling pointer errors with these entry objects, corroborating the race.
attack_scenario: Two threads concurrently operate on the same COMPOUND_RBT transliterator ID: Thread A calls Transliterator::createBasicInstance(), obtains a TransliteratorAlias holding a raw compoundFilter pointer from registry->get(), releases registryMutex, and begins alias->create() which calls compoundFilter->clone(); simultaneously, Thread B calls Transliterator::unregister() which acquires the mutex, calls registry->remove(), and triggers deleteEntry() that frees the entry including compoundFilter ‚ÄĒ leaving Thread A's alias with a dangling pointer and producing a use-after-free in compoundFilter->clone().

proof_trace: At L1557, `uprv_free(p)` is called where `p` is a `char**` traversal pointer initialized to `regions`; on the first iteration this frees the `regions` array itself, and `uprv_free(regions)` at L1559 double-frees the same block. Subsequent iterations call `uprv_free` on interior (non-heap-start) addresses of the already-freed block, corrupting heap metadata. The destructor (~TZDBNames, L1466‚Äď1470) uses the correct `uprv_free(*p)` (dereference) pattern, confirming L1557 is a typo where `*p` was written as `p`.
attack_scenario: An attacker who can supply or trigger loading of a timezone resource bundle containing `parseRegions` entries where `ures_getStringByIndex` returns failure (malformed resource data) sets `regionError=TRUE`, triggering the double-free path via any ICU timezone/date formatting API (e.g., `TZDBTimeZoneNames::getMetaZoneNames` ‚Üí `TZDBNames::createInstance`), leading to heap corruption and potential code execution.
